---
title: "Resources"
description: "Reusable code, protocols, and teaching materials for plant ecophysiology research."
---

A curated collection of tools, code, and protocols that support my research workflow and that collaborators have found useful.

## Code and analysis

**Hydraulic vulnerability curve fitting** — R package for fitting and comparing vulnerability curves from bench dehydration experiments. Includes confidence interval estimation and published comparison functions.
[Repository on GitHub](https://github.com/duojianghuji/) | [Documentation](https://duojianghuji.github.io/)

**Chlorophyll fluorescence image processing** — Python pipeline for batch processing PAM fluorescence images, extracting spatial heterogeneity in quenching rates, and generating publication-ready figures.
[GitHub](https://github.com/duojianghuji/) | Status: Active development

**Water potential modeling** — R code for inverse modeling plant water status using predawn and midday measurements; includes soil-plant-atmosphere continuum (SPAC) parameterization.
[GitHub](https://github.com/duojianghuji/)

## Data and protocols

**Standard protocols for pressure chamber measurements** — Step-by-step documentation for obtaining accurate water potential, xylem conductivity, and root hydraulic conductivity. Includes troubleshooting and quality control checkpoints.
[Protocols.io](https://www.protocols.io/)

**Vulnerability curve determination guide** — Visual protocol for the bench dehydration method. Covers branch selection, measurement timing, and common pitfalls.
[Protocols.io](https://www.protocols.io/)

**Plant hydraulic traits database (n = 200+ species)** — Compiled values for xylem pressure at 50% conductivity loss (P₅₀), stomatal conductance under different water stress regimes, and photosynthetic parameters. Data from published literature.
[Figshare](https://figshare.com/) | [Metadata](https://osf.io/)

**Soil-plant water relations dataset (semi-arid woodlands)** — Raw measurements from 2022–2024 field campaigns including predawn water potential, stomatal conductance, soil matric potential, and sap flux density.
[Open Science Framework (OSF)](https://osf.io/)

## Talks and teaching

**"Plant hydraulic strategy and ecosystem drought risk"** — Invited seminar slides (ASPP 2024). Summarizes the current understanding of how species-level hydraulic traits scale to ecosystem vulnerability.
[Slides on Figshare](https://figshare.com/)

**"Chlorophyll fluorescence as a diagnostic tool"** — Short tutorial on interpreting Fv/Fm, non-photochemical quenching, and photochemical efficiency in the context of drought stress. Aimed at graduate students new to fluorescence methods.
[Tutorial notebook on OSF](https://osf.io/)

**Resources for teaching plant physiology** — Curated links to open-access tutorials, video demonstrations of pressure chamber use, and interactive visualization tools.
- [Plant-water relations simulator](https://example.com/)
- [Photosynthesis animation](https://example.com/)
- [Stomatal regulation models](https://example.com/)
